title: Always evaluate the adapted model against its base
description: Compare the fine-tuned model to the base on both the target task and a general-capability holdout to catch regressions.
body: Use when judging whether a fine-tune is worth shipping. Do not report only the task metric — a model can gain task accuracy while losing general reasoning, instruction following, or safety behavior. Actions: run the fine-tuned and base models on the task eval and a fixed capability/safety holdout, then compare deltas on both. Validation signals include a benchmark table showing a task gain with no material capability regression and a recorded base-vs-tuned comparison. Risk boundary: shipping a model that quietly lost capabilities harms unrelated use cases; if the capability holdout regresses, deny release and escalate to rebalance the data or method.
